behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real embedding-model output shape crossing into the vector-index contract. A unit/integration fake can hardcode a correctly sized vector and miss misconfigured model selection or provider-side dimensionality changes. REAL-INFRA may be present, but the key risk is dependence on a real AI/ML call. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior text is about one model output contract check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test against the real embedding endpoint configured for production model ID, generate an embedding for a fixed query, fetch expected index dimension from the real index schema/config, and assert exact dimension match. Include a negative config variant (wrong model/index pairing) to verify the test fails on true mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/contract correctness between search service logic and vector-search component. It does not require real OS/network/subprocess if exercised with an in-process vector index or local test DB and deterministic vectors. Rejected higher level System: behavior text does not require real network or subprocess semantics. Rejected Agentic: correctness here is score ordering logic, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test index with deterministic vectors and known cosine relationships, run similarity search through the real service/repository wiring, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly validates model-driven ranking judgment for nuanced intent; stubs/mocks would always pass or fail deterministically and cannot catch real LLM decision failures. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior text specifies a single LLM re-ranking decision point, not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a fixed candidate set where lexical similarity ranking is known to conflict with nuanced intent, call the real re-ranker model, and assert at least one position changes from raw ranking in repeated runs with acceptance thresholds to handle nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Whether a query is semantically 'close' depends on embedding-model output behavior plus thresholding. Deterministic stubs can hide real semantic false positives and calibration failures. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior centers on one model-dependent retrieval decision boundary, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a realistic corpus and real embedding/search path, issue out-of-domain queries expected to have no semantic neighbors, and assert empty results under production thresholding (with guardrails on max score and returned count).

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is full-pipeline SLO validation across multiple agentic steps in sequence (embedding call then LLM re-rank) plus retrieval/orchestration. Isolated system tests of single components miss cross-step interaction and compounded latency distribution. Rejected lower levels: Integration cannot capture real infra timing; System at single-boundary scope is insufficient for multi-agentic-step end-to-end median behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end load test against the live API path with real embedding and re-ranking model calls and real vector store, collect per-request latency over a representative query set, and assert median (p50) total latency < 2.0s.
